source_pr.title: planner: ignore index_merge when partial path has global index
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:

2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--
ignore index merge when partial path is global index.

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: ref #43013 

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

### Release note

symptoms: No concrete flaky failure signature recorded in the case data; PR updates planner logic (index merge path selection) and enables additional test assertions.
failure_signature: No concrete flaky signature recorded; planner fix + test assertion updates.
root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: This PR changes planner behavior (ignore index_merge when partial path has a global index) and updates tests. The captured case evidence is not a clear flaky signature (the shown SQL already has ORDER BY), so keep it as insufficient evidence rather than mislabeling it as ORDER BY-related flakiness.
fix_pattern: Planner fix for index merge path selection with global index; no clearly evidenced anti-flake stabilization in the case data.

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("select a from t partition (p0) order by a").Check(testkit.Rows("abc", "acd")) (executor/test/writetest/write_test.go)

Changed files (3): executor/partition_table_test.go, executor/test/writetest/write_test.go, planner/core/indexmerge_path.go
Patch series analyzed; see patch_url.
